title: convolvulus supinus subsp. brevipes
intro: Convolvulus supinus subsp. brevipes, also known as the creeping convolvulus or bindweed, is a perennial flowering plant in the morning glory family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, where it is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Convolvulus supinus subsp. brevipes include its potential to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
